options: the drag will be multiplied by 4.
the drag will be multiplied by 16.
the drag will be multiplied by 8.

the drag will be multiplied by 9.
the drag will be multiplied by 6.
the drag will be multiplied by 3.

options: the drag will be divided by 4.

the drag will be multiplied by 4.

options: the point where the airflow is divided between the upper and lower wing surfaces.
the point of application of the resultant aerodynamic force.
the point where the airflow separates from the wing.
the point where the airflow on the wing upper surface rejoins the flow from the lower surface.

66.text: When a glider is flown with maximum load, the point of stall
options: occurs at the same speed and angle of attack independent of load.
occurs at a lower speed than with minimum load.
occurs at a higher speed than with minimum loading.

67.text: When a glider is flown with minimum load, the point of stall occurs
options: at the same speed and angle of attack independent of load.
at a lower speed than with maximum load.
at a higher speed than with maximum load.
at a smaller angle of attack.

69.text: If the positive angle of attack of a wing in an airflow is increased, the stagnation point moves
options: along the wing upper surface towards the trailing edge.
along the wing lower surface towards the leading edge.
along the wing lower surface towards the trailing edge.
along the wing upper surface towards the leading edge.

73.text: The term "average chord" is defined as
options: the average distance between the leading and trailing edges of a wing.
the difference in angle of attack of the wing cross-section at different points along the wing.
the distance between both wing tips.
the average loading per unit of wing surface area.

74.text: The term "wing loading" is defined as
options: the difference in angle of attack of the wing cross-section at different points along the wing.
the average distance between the leading and trailing edges of a wing.
the distance between the left and right wing tips.
the average loading per unit of wing surface area.

77.text: The aspect ratio is calculated in the following way
options: average chord x wing surface area.

78.text: Wings with a large aspect ratio have
options: a small span and a small average chord.
a large span and a small average chord
a large span and a large average chord.
a small span and a large average chord.

The sink rate and the glide ratio increase.
Its glide ratio will reduce from 10 to 5.
Its glide ratio will increase from 5 to 10.
